source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.source_statement: Draw inferences such as inferring characters' feelings, thoughts and motives from their actions, and justify inferences with evidence.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack focuses on making an inference and selecting text evidence that supports it.

objective.child_goal: I can use clues in the text to prove an inference.
objective.statement: Infer meaning from a text and justify the inference with evidence.
misconceptions: Gives an opinion without text evidence.
Chooses evidence that is interesting but does not support the inference.
Copies a quote without explaining what it shows.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A sensible idea worked out from clues in the text.
vocabulary.audio_script: An inference is what you work out from clues.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A clue beam connecting a sentence to a thought bubble.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Words from the text that prove or support your idea.
vocabulary.audio_script: Evidence is the part of the text that supports your answer.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Highlighted words feeding a proof meter.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Tap the words that tell us what happened.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Separate retrieval from inference before asking for hidden meaning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: First find what the text says clearly.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A short extract with explicit facts highlighted.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The city scanner can only power up when your idea has text evidence.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce inference as idea plus proof, not guessing.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A good inference needs a clue from the text.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A thought bubble connects to highlighted text through a beam.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Mira kept checking the door. That clue suggests she felt nervous.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model linking action evidence to a feeling inference.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The action is the clue. Checking the door again and again can show nervousness.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The phrase kept checking the door is highlighted and linked to nervous.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Highlight the words that best prove your inference.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise evidence selection rather than broad copying.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Learner drags a highlight over exact words; proof meter fills only for relevant evidence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which clue best proves that the character is worried?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect whether the learner picks relevant evidence.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The best evidence must prove the idea.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Nova how your highlighted words prove your answer.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to connect evidence to inference.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you tell me what the clue shows?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion asks for the because part of the answer.

manipulatives.purpose: Let learners select precise text evidence and connect it to an inference.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners highlight phrases, choose an inference and write or select the because explanation.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard text selection, audio read-aloud and reduced-highlight visual mode are supported.

manipulatives.purpose: Develop strategic inference by weighing two plausible quotations and justifying which provides the stronger evidence.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners inspect paired evidence cards, rank their relevance, reject an attractive but weaker clue and build a concise inference-evidence-because verdict.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Cards use numbered labels rather than position alone; every action supports keyboard control and single-switch scanning, with no-drag buttons, audio read-aloud, a static comparison table and a reduced-motion mode with no animated linking or reordering.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave feelings, motives and setting inference only after evidence relevance is stable.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Select precise evidence for character feelings in short extracts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: extract + inference stem + exact supporting phrase
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Text extracts must be age-appropriate and culturally safe.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose evidence that supports motive rather than merely interesting detail.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: motive question + four evidence options with one best support
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distractors should be plausible but clearly less relevant.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain how a quote supports an inference using because language.
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Accept varied phrasing; scoring should look for evidence connection, not exact wording.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Provide spaced review across narrative, explanation and persuasive extracts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review extract + one inference + evidence highlight
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Control vocabulary load so the task assesses inference, not decoding.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Offer equivalent evidence practice without animated proof beams.
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check readability, line spacing and keyboard navigation.

body.prompt: Highlight the words that show Mira is nervous.
body.extract: Mira kept checking the door and twisted the ribbon in her hand.
body.inference: Mira is nervous.

expected_answer.value: kept checking the door

hints: Look for an action that gives a clue.
Which words show she cannot relax?
question_variants.explanation: Kept checking the door suggests Mira is nervous because she is watching for something.

body.prompt: Which evidence best shows that Leo is proud?
choices: Leo stood taller when the model worked.
The model had four wheels.
It was raining outside.
Leo wore a blue coat.

expected_answer.value: Leo stood taller when the model worked.

hints: Proud is a feeling.
Choose the clue that shows Leo's feeling.
question_variants.explanation: Standing taller after success is the best clue that Leo feels proud.

body.prompt: Explain how the quote proves that Asha is determined: Asha tried again, even though her hands were shaking.

expected_answer.value: It shows Asha is determined because she tried again even though she was nervous or finding it hard.

hints: Use the word because.
Explain what tried again shows.
question_variants.explanation: Tried again shows determination because Asha keeps going even though it is hard.

body.prompt: Evidence highlighter route: highlight the most precise clue supporting 'Leena feels worried or cautious.'.
body.extract: Leena checked the tide chart for the third time. She tightened the rope around the little boat, although the water was still calm.
chunks: Leena checked the tide chart for the third time.
She tightened the rope around the little boat, although the water was still calm.
body.inference: Leena feels worried or cautious.
body.representation: chunked text with keyboard-selectable evidence spans
body.static_alternative: chunked extract, numbered sentences, quote bank and one question on screen
body.reduced_motion_alternative: instant highlights with no proof beams, countdown or animated ranking
sentence_stems: I infer ___ because the text says ___.

expected_answer.value: checked the tide chart for the third time

hints: Find an action, image or wording that reveals the feeling without naming it.
Choose the smallest sufficient phrase rather than the whole extract.
question_variants.explanation: 'checked the tide chart for the third time' supports leena feels worried or cautious. because the action or image is a precise behavioural clue.

feedback.correct: Evidence trail verified. 'checked the tide chart for the third time' supports leena feels worried or cautious. because the action or image is a precise behavioural clue.
feedback.try_again: No timer and no lost progress. Find an action, image or wording that reveals the feeling without naming it.
feedback.misconception: The 'opinion without evidence' route needs a closer clue or a more cautious claim. Choose the smallest sufficient phrase rather than the whole extract.
feedback.strategy: Use inference -> exact clue -> what the clue suggests, with sentence-by-sentence highlighting.
feedback.quote_explain_check: Select the smallest sufficient quote, then explain what its wording reveals.
feedback.sufficiency_check: Ask whether the evidence is relevant, precise and enough for the strength of the claim.
feedback.interpretation_check: A second interpretation may be plausible; compare exact clues and state what remains unknown.

gamification.mission: Recover the feelings clue from the narrative file
gamification.objective: Restore one story-case file by linking a precise clue to a cautious inference.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Gives an opinion without text evidence.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects an inference but no supporting phrase.
repair_sequence: Ask what words in the text gave the idea.
Connect chosen clue to the inference with a because frame.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects a relevant phrase for a new inference.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses evidence that is interesting but does not support the inference.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects a vivid detail unrelated to the inference.
repair_sequence: Restate the inference as a question.
Compare two evidence choices and ask which proves it.
Highlight only the phrase that answers the question.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects evidence that directly supports the inference.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Copies a quote without explaining what it shows.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner gives evidence but no because explanation.
repair_sequence: Show quote plus inference as two separate cards.
Ask learner to complete what the quote shows.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner writes or selects a clear because explanation.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use static highlights and calm proof-meter states.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace evidence beams with outline connectors.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read extracts aloud with replay and line focus.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use chunked extracts, wider line spacing and vocabulary preteach.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Ask one inference and one evidence decision at a time.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Treat evidence revision as detective work rather than wrong answers.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach feeling and motive vocabulary with examples.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires a plausible inference with relevant evidence; secure requires precise evidence and explanation across mixed short extracts.
evidence.parent_summary: Can work out what a text suggests and choose words that prove the idea.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Links inferences to precise evidence and explains how the evidence supports the answer.
next_actions: Add motive and viewpoint inference once feeling evidence is secure.
Develop written because explanations into full reading responses.

qa.notes: Year 5 reading inference and evidence reaches the 220-item pilot target with three preserved curated variants and 217 deterministic multi-genre review candidates. Coverage includes clue retrieval, feelings, motives, viewpoint, vocabulary implications, prediction, evidence sufficiency and precision, competing interpretations, overclaim repair, quote-and-explain and transfer. Every generated item uses original age-appropriate extracts, dyslexia/SEND chunking, supported evidence highlighting, rich feedback, optional human-reviewed ElevenLabs references with browser TTS prohibited, and private detective/story missions without timers, lives, streak pressure, leaderboards or peer comparison. Independent English, teacher, SEND, accessibility, safeguarding, audio, renderer and pilot review remain required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
